factors.summary: Demand is accelerating, particularly in pool and residential water treatment businesses.

evidence.text: The high level of demand in these businesses continued as consumers invested in their homes and backyards. Demand in our commercial water treatment business improved in 2021 as the restaurant and hospitality industries reopened.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: The summary indicates accelerating demand in key businesses, supported by evidence of strong consumer and commercial demand.

factors.summary: The company is selectively increasing prices to mitigate inflationary costs.

evidence.text: • selective increases in selling prices to mitigate a rise in inflationary costs; and

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: The company is strategically increasing prices to mitigate inflationary costs, which is a positive response to cost pressures.

factors.summary: Organic revenue growth is a focus, with strong demand across product lines.

evidence.text: Our Consumer Solutions segment delivered significant growth in the first nine months of 2021 led by continued strength in our pool and residential water treatment businesses.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Strong organic revenue growth is evident across product lines, supported by significant growth in key segments.

factors.summary: Volume trends are growing, driven by strong demand in both consumer and industrial segments.

evidence.text: The 21.4 and 25.0 percent increases in net sales in the third quarter and first nine months, respectively, of 2021 from 2020 were primarily driven by: • volume increase in our Consumer Solutions segment mainly driven by continued robust demand in our pool and residential water treatment businesses; • volume increase in our Industrial & Flow Technologies segment primarily driven by strong demand across all product lines in the residential and irrigation flow businesses as well as increased demand in sustainable gas solutions and recovery in short cycle orders in our food and beverage business;

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Volume trends are growing strongly in both consumer and industrial segments, driven by robust demand.

factors.summary: End markets are growing fastest in consumer and industrial segments, particularly in pool and water treatment products.

evidence.text: Our Consumer Solutions segment delivered significant growth in the first nine months of 2021 led by continued strength in our pool and residential water treatment businesses. The high level of demand in these businesses continued as consumers invested in their homes and backyards. Demand in our commercial water treatment business improved in 2021 as the restaurant and hospitality industries reopened.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: End markets are growing fastest in consumer and industrial segments, particularly in pool and water treatment products, indicating strong demand.

factors.summary: Input costs are rising due to inflationary pressures, particularly in raw materials and logistics.

evidence.text: • inflationary cost increases due to tight supply of raw materials such as metals, resins and electronics; and • higher logistics and labor costs due to increased demand as well as factory labor wage increases.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Input costs are rising due to inflationary pressures, which could negatively impact profitability.

factors.summary: Labor costs are increasing, contributing to margin pressure.

evidence.text: • higher logistics and labor costs due to increased demand as well as factory labor wage increases.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Labor costs are increasing, contributing to margin pressure, which is a negative development for the company.

factors.summary: Gross and operating margins are expanding despite cost pressures.

evidence.text: The 0.2 percentage point decrease in gross profit as a percentage of net sales in the third quarter of 2021 from 2020 was primarily driven by: • inflationary cost increases due to tight supply of raw materials such as metals, resins and electronics; and • higher logistics and labor costs due to increased demand as well as factory labor wage increases. This decrease was partially offset by: • volume expansion in both our Consumer Solutions and Industrial & Flow Technologies segments; • selective increases in selling prices to mitigate impacts of inflation; and • increased productivity in both the Consumer Solutions and Industrial & Flow Technologies segments.

sentiment.label: very_positive
sentiment.rationale: Despite cost pressures, gross and operating margins are expanding due to volume growth, price increases, and productivity improvements.

factors.summary: Cost reduction initiatives are ongoing, with transformation costs incurred in the first nine months of 2021.

evidence.text: During the first nine months of 2021, we incurred transformation costs that primarily represented professional services and project management related charges. Throughout the remainder of 2021 and beyond we expect to continue to incur transformation costs that include professional services, project management and related design and execution charges, as well as costs related to both labor and non-labor restructuring and IT investments.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: The company is actively pursuing cost reduction initiatives, which are expected to continue and contribute to margin expansion.

factors.summary: Productivity is improving, contributing to margin expansion.

evidence.text: The 0.9 percentage point increase in gross profit as a percentage of net sales in the first nine months of 2021 from 2020 was primarily driven by: • volume expansion in both our Consumer Solutions and Industrial & Flow Technologies segments; • selective increases in selling prices to mitigate impacts of inflation; and • increased productivity in both the Consumer Solutions and Industrial & Flow Technologies segments.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Productivity improvements are contributing to margin expansion, indicating a positive trend.

factors.summary: Management is confident, with actions including buybacks and investments in growth.

evidence.text: Our operating objectives remain to focus on delivering our core while continuing to build out our future. We expect to execute these objectives by: • Delivering revenue growth in our core businesses; • Delivering income and cash by managing price/cost inflation, prioritization of growth investments and addressing the cost structures as necessary; • Continued focus on capital allocation through: ◦ Commitment to maintain our investment grade rating; ◦ Return cash to shareholders through dividends and buybacks; and ◦ Supplement our business with strategically-aligned mergers and acquisitions.
evidence.subsection: Key Trends and Uncertainties Regarding Our Existing Business

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Management is confident in executing growth strategies, including buybacks and strategic investments, which are positive signals for the company's outlook.

factors.summary: Near-term risks include supply chain challenges and inflationary pressures.

evidence.text: • During the first nine months of 2021, we experienced supply chain challenges, including increased lead times for raw materials due to availability constraints and high demand. While we have elevated our engagement with our suppliers and used secondary suppliers and new methods of procurement where available to mitigate the supply chain pressures, we expect supply chain challenges to continue for the remainder of 2021, and which may continue thereafter and could negatively impact our results of operations. • During the first nine months of 2021, we experienced inflationary increases of raw materials such as metals, resins and electronics, as well as increases in logistics and labor costs. While we have taken pricing actions and we strive for productivity improvements that could help offset these inflationary cost increases, we expect inflationary cost increases to continue for the remainder of 2021, and which may continue thereafter and could negatively impact our results of operations.
evidence.subsection: Key Trends and Uncertainties Regarding Our Existing Business

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Near-term risks such as supply chain challenges and inflationary pressures could negatively impact operations, indicating a headwind.

factors.summary: Long-term strategy includes transformation initiatives to drive growth and margin expansion.

evidence.text: We are targeting at least 300 basis points of margin expansion by 2025 through the following Transformation Program pillars: • reducing business and product complexity; • developing a future state digital enterprise by eliminating non-value add activities, streamlining processes and using automation to increase productivity; • modernizing general and administrative expenses; and • delivering decision making speed through improved processes and analytics.

sentiment.label: very_positive
sentiment.rationale: The company has a clear long-term strategy with specific targets for margin expansion through transformation initiatives, signaling significant positive developments.

factors.summary: Key sensitivities include raw material costs, supply chain disruptions, and labor costs.

evidence.text: • inflationary cost increases due to tight supply of raw materials such as metals, resins, and electronics; and • higher logistics and labor costs due to increased demand as well as factory labor wage increases.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: The company identifies key sensitivities such as raw material costs and labor costs, but these are presented as risks without clear directional sentiment.

factors.summary: Catalysts include the Pleatco acquisition and transformation program.

evidence.text: On September 15, 2021, as part of both of our Consumer Solutions and Industrial & Flow Technologies reporting segments, we entered into a definitive agreement to acquire Pleatco Holdings, LLC and related entities (“Pleatco”), a manufacturer of water filtration and clean air technologies for pool, spa and industrial air customers, for total consideration of approximately $255.0 million in cash, subject to customary adjustments. We completed the Pleatco acquisition on October 18, 2021. On May 19, 2021, as part of our Consumer Solutions reporting segment, we completed the acquisition of Ken’s Beverage, Inc. (“KBI”) for $82.8 million in cash, net of cash acquired. KBI provides beverage equipment and services to commercial customers.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Recent acquisitions and transformation programs indicate positive catalysts for growth and expansion.

factors.summary: R&D investment is increasing, with focus on innovation and productivity.

evidence.text: Research and development expenses 64.4 55.6 8.8 15.8 % •     % of net sales 2.3 % 2.5 % (0.2) pts

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Increased R&D investment suggests a focus on innovation and future growth, which is a positive signal.

factors.summary: Digital automation is part of the transformation program to drive operational excellence.

evidence.text: • developing a future state digital enterprise by eliminating non-value add activities, streamlining processes and using automation to increase productivity;

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Evidence shows automation is being used to increase productivity and streamline processes.

factors.summary: New products are being launched, with the Pleatco acquisition adding filtration technologies.

evidence.text: On September 15, 2021, as part of both of our Consumer Solutions and Industrial & Flow Technologies reporting segments, we entered into a definitive agreement to acquire Pleatco Holdings, LLC and related entities (“Pleatco”), a manufacturer of water filtration and clean air technologies for pool, spa and industrial air customers, for total consideration of approximately $255.0 million in cash, subject to customary adjustments. We completed the Pleatco acquisition on October 18, 2021.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Acquisition of Pleatco adds filtration technologies, indicating product expansion.

factors.summary: Economic conditions are a tailwind with strong demand and stimulus measures.

evidence.text: The COVID-19 pandemic continues to persist throughout the United States (“U.S.”) and the world, with the continued potential for significant impact. The COVID-19 pandemic has resulted in governments around the world implementing stringent measures to help control the spread of the virus, including quarantines, “shelter-in-place” and “stay-at-home” orders, travel restrictions, business curtailments, limits on gatherings, vaccine and mask requirements, and other measures. In addition, governments and central banks in several parts of the world have enacted fiscal and monetary stimulus measures to counteract the economic impacts of the COVID-19 pandemic.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Ongoing pandemic impacts and measures indicate challenging economic conditions.

factors.summary: Supply chain health is a concern with ongoing challenges and increased lead times.

evidence.text: • During the first nine months of 2021, we experienced supply chain challenges, including increased lead times for raw materials due to availability constraints and high demand. While we have elevated our engagement with our suppliers and used secondary suppliers and new methods of procurement where available to mitigate the supply chain pressures, we expect supply chain challenges to continue for the remainder of 2021, and which may continue thereafter and could negatively impact our results of operations.
evidence.subsection: Key Trends and Uncertainties Regarding Our Existing Business

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Supply chain challenges with increased lead times and potential negative impacts.

factors.summary: CapEx is focused on growth and operational efficiency.

evidence.text: • Focused growth initiatives that accelerate our investments in digital, technology and services expansion;
evidence.subsection: Key Trends and Uncertainties Regarding Our Existing Business

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Investments in digital, technology, and services expansion suggest growth.

factors.summary: Debt leverage is improving with lower debt levels and interest rates.

evidence.text: The 51.9 and 42.5 percent decreases in net interest expense in the third quarter and first nine months, respectively, of 2021 from 2020 were primarily driven by: • strong cash flows in 2021 used to repay senior fixed notes that matured as well as reduce variable debt leading to overall lower debt levels compared to the same periods of the prior year; and • lower interest rates on outstanding variable debt.

sentiment.label: very_positive
sentiment.rationale: Significant decrease in net interest expense due to lower debt levels and rates.

factors.summary: Shareholder returns are a focus, with plans for dividends and buybacks.

evidence.text: • Return cash to shareholders through dividends and buybacks;
evidence.subsection: Key Trends and Uncertainties Regarding Our Existing Business

sentiment.rationale: Plans for dividends and buybacks indicate returning value to shareholders.

We completed the Pleatco acquisition on October 18, 2021. On May 19, 2021, as part of our Consumer Solutions reporting segment, we completed the acquisition of Ken’s Beverage, Inc. (“KBI”) for $82.8 million in cash, net of cash acquired. KBI provides beverage equipment and services to commercial customers.
